WidocoPublishingService.name: Generate documentation with Widoco
WidocoPublishingService.title: Generate documentation with Widoco
WidocoPublishingService.description:
[Wizard for documenting ontologies](https://github.com/dgarijo/Widoco). WIDOCO is a step by step generator of HTML templates with the documentation of your ontology. It uses the [LODE](https://github.com/essepuntato/LODE) environment to create part of the template.

The options below allows to customize the supported options.

ANALYTICS.title: Google Analytics ID
ANALYTICS.description: Add a code snippet for Google analytics to track your HTML documentation. You need to add your CODE next to the flag. For example: UA-1234

CONF_FILE.title: Configuration file
CONF_FILE.description: Load your own configuration file for the ontology metadata. Use this option if you want to load your own HTML sections as well. Incompatible with `-getOntologyMetadata`. See the [configuration documentation](https://github.com/dgarijo/Widoco/blob/v1.4.25/doc/configuration/configuration_doc.md) for more information about the accepted fields.

DISPLAY_DIRECT_IMPORTS_ONLY.title: Display direct imports only
DISPLAY_DIRECT_IMPORTS_ONLY.description: Only those imported ontologies that are directly imported in the ontology being documented.

DO_NOT_DISPLAY_SERIALIZATIONS.title: Do not display serializations
DO_NOT_DISPLAY_SERIALIZATIONS.description: The serializations of the ontology will not be displayed.

EXCLUDE_INTRODUCTION.title: Exclude introduction
EXCLUDE_INTRODUCTION.description: Skip the introduction section in the documentation.

EXCLUDE_PROVENANCE.title: Exclude provenance
EXCLUDE_PROVENANCE.description: Do not add the link "Provenance of this page" in the metadata header section

IGNORE_INDIVIDUALS.title: Ignore individuals
IGNORE_INDIVIDUALS.description: Individuals will not be included in the documentation.

INCLUDE_ANNOTATION_PROPERTIES.title: Include annotation properties
INCLUDE_ANNOTATION_PROPERTIES.description: Include annotation properties defined in your ontology in the documentation (by default they are not included).

INCLUDE_IMPORTED_ONTOLOGIES.title: Include imported ontologies
INCLUDE_IMPORTED_ONTOLOGIES.description: Indicates whether the terms of the imported ontologies of the current ontology should be documented as well or not.

IMPORT.title: Import local ontology
IMPORT.description: Imports a local ontology (e.g., if you don't want to load an online ontology, you may load its local version)

LANG.title: Languages
LANG.description: Generate documentation in multiple languages. If the language is not supported, the system will load the labels in english. Note that only basic language tags not containing "-" are supported due to Widoco limitations (instead of en-US, only en must be used).

LICENSIUS.title: Licensius
LICENSIUS.description: Use the Licensius web services (http://licensius.com/apidoc/index.html) to retrieve license metadata. Only works if the -getOntologyMetadata flag is enabled.

NO_PLACEHOLDER_TEXT.title: No placeholder text
NO_PLACEHOLDER_TEXT.description: Do not add any placeholder text (this will remove intro, abstract (if empty) and description sections).

ONT_FILE.title: Ontology file
ONT_FILE.description: Load a local ontology file to document.

OOPS.title: OOPS!
OOPS.description: Create an html page with the evaluation from the OntOlogy Pitfall Scanner! service (http://oops.linkeddata.es/)

UNITE_SECTIONS.title: Unite sections
UNITE_SECTIONS.description: Write all HTML sections into a single HTML document.

USE_CUSTOM_STYLE.title: Use custom style
USE_CUSTOM_STYLE.description: Export the documentation using **alternate** css files (by Daniel Vila).

WEB_VOWL.title: WebVowl
WEB_VOWL.description: Create a graph visualization based on [WebVowl](https://github.com/VisualDataWeb/WebVOWL) in the documentation.

RedirectSerializationToOntopus.title: Redirect serialization to Ontopus
RedirectSerializationToOntopus.description: Removes serializations generated by Widoco and redirects them to standard OntoPuS endpoint.
